# xcaffold

> Deterministic agent configuration compiler. Compiles .xcaf YAML into native configurations for AI coding platforms.

xcaffold is an open-source CLI tool that lets teams define agent configurations once in `.xcaf` files and compile them to native formats for any supported AI coding platform. It follows the Agent-as-Code (AaC) paradigm — agent configurations are version-controlled, reviewed, and deployed like infrastructure code.

## Key Concepts

- `.xcaf` files: YAML manifests defining agents, skills, rules, hooks, memory, MCP servers, and project settings
- Compilation: deterministic transformation from `.xcaf` source to provider-native output
- Providers: target platforms — each has a renderer that emits the platform's native format
- Drift detection: compare compiled output against filesystem state
- Blueprints: named configuration profiles for different environments or team roles
- Fidelity notes: warnings when a provider cannot fully represent a source configuration

## Commands

- `xcaffold init` — scaffold a new project with project.xcaf
- `xcaffold apply` — compile .xcaf source to provider-native output
- `xcaffold diff` — show differences between compiled and on-disk state
- `xcaffold import` — ingest existing provider configurations as .xcaf source
- `xcaffold validate` — parse and validate .xcaf files without compiling
- `xcaffold graph` — render the dependency graph for a project
- `xcaffold list` — enumerate compiled resources by kind and provider
- `xcaffold status` — show drift between source and output

## Resource Kinds

- `agent` — AI assistant persona with model, tools, and instructions
- `skill` — reusable task workflow with steps and references
- `rule` — behavioral constraint or coding standard
- `hook` — shell command triggered by lifecycle events
- `memory` — persistent knowledge attached to an agent
- `mcp` — Model Context Protocol server configuration
- `context` — shared instruction block composable across agents
- `project` — project-level settings and variables

## Links

- Source: https://github.com/saero-ai/xcaffold
- License: Apache-2.0
